headlines.title: President Trump vows revenge on Iran for killing U.S. service members
headlines.description: After a weekend of deadly attacks, the Pentagon has released the identities of some of the fallen service members, including 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan from Hawaii and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales from Texas, who were killed from an Iranian attack. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports from the region.

headlines.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
headlines.description: The Pentagon identified the soldiers as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: Judge temporarily pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. deal
headlines.description: A federal judge temporarily paused the proposed merger of Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery, granting a request from a coalition of 12 state attorneys general who sued to thwart the $110 billion deal.

headlines.title: Tate brothers in Miami court after new U.K. charges
headlines.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared in a Miami courtroom after British prosecutors unveiled a wave of new charges, including rape. Authorities from the U.K. are seeking to extradite the Tate brothers. NBC News' Danny Cevallos reports on the 38 charges they face.

headlines.title: FDA says it’s still investigating Taylor Farms in cyclosporiasis outbreak
headlines.description: Taylor Farms continues to be the subject of a Food and Drug Administration investigation into the growing cyclosporiasis outbreak, despite a false positive test result, the agency said Monday.

headlines.title: Andy Burnham formally appointed U.K. prime minster
headlines.description: Andy Burnham was formally appointed the newest British prime minster, the seventh to take up the position in the last decade. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports on how Burnham differs from outgoing prime minster Keir Starmer and his meeting with King Charles III.

headlines.title: A look at the most exciting moments from the 2026 FIFA World Cup
headlines.description: A photo gallery of highlights from World Cup 2026.
headlines.source: NBC News Digital Art and Photo Department

headlines.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
headlines.description: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: Father, son charged with kidnapping 2 U.S. Forest Service biologists at gunpoint
headlines.description: Joseph and Phoenix Henrichsen were charged with kidnapping a federal employee. “I’ve taken two fed biologists hostage,” the father is accused of saying in a voicemail.

headlines.title: Democrats avoid a messy fight in Maine, but uncertainty lingers: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.
